chunks.text: Stereo. H C J D A 38 JUDGMENT SHEET IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT JUDGMENT W.P.No.29214/2011 University of Health Sciences, Lahore VS. Government of Pakistan through the Secretary, Ministry of Inter Provincial Coordination, Islamabad etc. Ch. Muhammad Iqbal, J:- Through this single judgment, I intend to decide the titled Writ Petition [No.29214/2011] and Writ Petition No.19848/2011 as common questions of law and facts are involved in these cases. Writ Petition No.29214/2011 2. Through this writ petition, the petitioner/University of Health Sciences, Lahore [hereinafter referred to as the ―UHS‖] has challenged the validity of notification dated 13.08.2011 issued by the Deputy Director General (Health), Ministry of Inter Provincial Coordination (Health Wing), Government of Pakistan. Date of hearing: 28.11.2022 Petitioners by: Barrister Ch. Muhammad Umar, Mufti Ihtisham-ud-Din and Rana Muhammad Ansar, Advocates Respondents by: Mr. Gohar Nawaz Sindhu, Additional Advocate General Mr. Imran Ahmad Malik, Advocate for respondents No.3 & 4 Sardar Taimoor Aslam Khan and Shahrukh Mehboob, Advocates for respondent No.5. Writ Petition No.29214/2011 2 3. Brief facts of this case are that the respondent No.3/The Superior College, Lahore was established under Section 3 of The Superior College Lahore Act 2004 (which was amended through The Superior College, Lahore (Amendment) Act, 2021‖ (XXIII of 2021) and named it as ―The Superior University, Lahore, Act, 2004‖ [hereinafter referred to as ―Act 2004‖] and was allowed to establish a medical college subject to fulfillment of the requirements laid down in Section 4 of the Act 2004 alongwith other legal applicable requirements under Section 37 of The University of Health Sciences Ordinance 2002 [hereinafter referred to as ―Ordinance 2002‖].

Writ Petition No.29214/2011 2 3. Brief facts of this case are that the respondent No.3/The Superior College, Lahore was established under Section 3 of The Superior College Lahore Act 2004 (which was amended through The Superior College, Lahore (Amendment) Act, 2021‖ (XXIII of 2021) and named it as ―The Superior University, Lahore, Act, 2004‖ [hereinafter referred to as ―Act 2004‖] and was allowed to establish a medical college subject to fulfillment of the requirements laid down in Section 4 of the Act 2004 alongwith other legal applicable requirements under Section 37 of The University of Health Sciences Ordinance 2002 [hereinafter referred to as ―Ordinance 2002‖]. Respondent No.3 established a medical college under the name & style of Azra Naheed Medical College [hereinafter referred to as ―College‖] (respondent No.4) and initiated proceedings to obtain recognition from the Pakistan Medical & Dental Council [hereinafter referred to as ―PMDC‖] (respondent No.5) under Section 11 of the Pakistan Medical and Dental Council Act, 1962. The College requested the UHS to issue NOC/Permission to grant affiliation vide letter dated 12.02.2011. The protracted correspondence was still underway between the UHS and the College but respondent No.1 vide impugned notification dated 13.08.2011 accorded approval to the Superior College, Lahore to impart medical education / training for MBBS degrees for 100 students. Through this petition the UHS contends that the impugned approval letter/notification is illegal and has been issued in violation of Section 37 of the Ordinance 2002. That the requirement of Section 4 of Act 2004 was not complied with by the College in letter and spirit, whereas being located within the territorial boundaries of the Province of Punjab, it was mandatory for the college to comply with the statutory requirements contained in Section 37 of the Ordinance 2002. Hence this writ petition. Writ Petition No.29214/2011 3 PMDC filed parawise comments and admitted that affiliation of the medical colleges with UHS was necessary and respondent No.1 also adopted the above comments, whereas respondents No.3 & 4 through filing parawise comments controverted the stance of the petitioner on legal and factual parlances and defended the impugned notification with the assertion that respondent No.1 was well within competence to allow the teaching of medical education to the students and there is no need to get affiliation with the Petitioner UHS and finally prayed for dismissal of this constitutional petition.

Hence this writ petition. Writ Petition No.29214/2011 3 PMDC filed parawise comments and admitted that affiliation of the medical colleges with UHS was necessary and respondent No.1 also adopted the above comments, whereas respondents No.3 & 4 through filing parawise comments controverted the stance of the petitioner on legal and factual parlances and defended the impugned notification with the assertion that respondent No.1 was well within competence to allow the teaching of medical education to the students and there is no need to get affiliation with the Petitioner UHS and finally prayed for dismissal of this constitutional petition. Writ Petition No.19848/2011 4. Through this writ petition, the Superior College, Lahore has challenged the validity of Notice dated 21.07.2011 whereby the UHS directed the petitioner to get Azra Naheed Medical College affiliated with UHS and press releases dated 25.06.2011 and 31.08.2011 issued by the UHS whereby the recognition of said College was declared as false and illegal. 5. Brief facts of this case are that the Superior University, Lahore established Azra Naheed Medical College under the provisions of the Act 2004 and filed an application to PMDC for grant of necessary approval for imparting medical education to the students in the said college. The petitioner was advised by the Higher Education Commission, Government of the Punjab to get affiliation of the college with the UHS. The petitioner-college approached UHS for said purpose. On 12.02.2011 UHS informed the Principal of Azra Naheed Medical College to firstly obtain necessary recognition from the PMDC whereafter the UHS shall grant affiliation. Later on, through notice dated 21.07.2011 the UHS again directed the petitioner to get the College affiliated with the UHS as required under Section 37 of the Ordinance 2002 and also advertised publications in daily ―Jang‖ dated 25.06.2011 Writ Petition No.29214/2011 4 and 31.08.2011 declaring the recognition of the Azra Naheed Medical College as false and illegal. The petitioner [Superior College] has prayed for setting aside of the aforementioned notices and publications. 6. I have heard the arguments of learned counsels for the parties and have gone through the record with their able assistance. 7.

The petitioner [Superior College] has prayed for setting aside of the aforementioned notices and publications. 6. I have heard the arguments of learned counsels for the parties and have gone through the record with their able assistance. 7. As per divergent pleadings of the litigating parties the following moot point is required to be answered: ―Whether after having obtained recognition certification under Section 11 of PMDC Act, 1962 the affiliation of medical teaching institutions located within the territorial boundaries of the Province of Punjab with UHS is mandatory under the Ordinance, 2002? Before delving into the above moot point, it is pertinent to analyse the legislative scheme of law on the subject. As per constitutional scheme, the legislative and executive domains of the federation as well as its provinces have been provided under Article 142, 143 & 144 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973 [hereinafter referred to as ―Constitution‖]. Under Article 70 of the Constitution, before 18th Constitutional amendment, there were two legislative lists i.e. (i) Federal Legislative List (the exclusive legislative domain of Federation) and (ii) concurrent Legislative list. In the concurrent legislative field Federation and Provinces, both had common legislative competence to make law and execute the same. The Federation promulgated Pakistan Medical and Dental Council Act, 1962 having legislative competence pursuant to entry No.38 of the concurrent list regarding Medical Education, through which PMDC inter alia to regulate/administer the affairs including recognition of the Medical colleges. Similarly the Provincial Government promulgated the Ordinance, 2002 in the Province of Punjab regarding affiliation of the Medical Colleges with UHS. Writ Petition No.29214/2011 5 Subsequently, 18th Amendment in the Constitution was promulgated in the year 2010 whereby the concurrent legislative list was abolished and consequently entry No.38 of concurrent list stood vested with the province(s). 8.

Similarly the Provincial Government promulgated the Ordinance, 2002 in the Province of Punjab regarding affiliation of the Medical Colleges with UHS. Writ Petition No.29214/2011 5 Subsequently, 18th Amendment in the Constitution was promulgated in the year 2010 whereby the concurrent legislative list was abolished and consequently entry No.38 of concurrent list stood vested with the province(s). 8. Now, adverting to the lis in hand and moot point, admittedly the Ordinance 2002 was promulgated on 28.09.2002 to establish University of Health Sciences as a regulatory body, inter alia to settle the matters of the medical teaching colleges / institutions throughout the province of Punjab. The controversy in the lis in hand is with regard to the condition of compulsory affiliation of the Medical Teaching Colleges or Institutions operating within the territorial limits of the province of Punjab with the UHS. The words ̳affiliated‘, ̳medical institution‘ and ̳university‘ have been defined in Section 2 of the Ordinance 2002 as under: ―2. Definitions.– In this Ordinance, unless there is anything repugnant in the subject or context:– (ii) ―affiliated‖ when used in relation to a medical college or institution means a college or institution affiliated to the university but not maintained or administered by it;..... (xviii) ―medical institution‖ means any school, institute, college or institution imparting medical education or teaching health sciences;..... (xxvii) ―University‖ means the University of Health Sciences, Lahore constituted under this Ordinance; Under Section 4 of the Ordinance 2002, powers of the UHS to present the courses, syllabus, affiliate, disaffiliate the colleges and medical institutions etc. are given. For reference, Section 4 is reproduced as under: ―4. Powers of the University.– The University shall have the powers to– (i) provide for instruction in such branches of learning, as it may deem fit and to make provision for research and for the advancement and dissemination of knowledge in such manner as it may determine; (ii) prescribe courses of studies to be taught in the medical institutions, affiliated and constituent colleges;

For reference, Section 4 is reproduced as under: ―4. Powers of the University.– The University shall have the powers to– (i) provide for instruction in such branches of learning, as it may deem fit and to make provision for research and for the advancement and dissemination of knowledge in such manner as it may determine; (ii) prescribe courses of studies to be taught in the medical institutions, affiliated and constituent colleges; Writ Petition No.29214/2011 6 (iii) hold examinations and to award and confer degrees, diplomas, certificates and other academic distinctions to and on persons who have been admitted to and have passed its examinations under prescribed conditions; (iv) confer, in the prescribed manner, honorary degrees or other academic distinctions on approved persons; (v) confer degrees on persons who have carried on independent research under prescribed conditions; (vi) provide for teaching specified courses of training for persons not being students of the University and grant certificates and diplomas to such persons; (vii) affiliate or disaffiliate colleges and other medical institutions in the prescribed manner; (viii) admit colleges and other educational institutions to its privileges or withdraw such privileges in the prescribed manner; ix) inspect colleges and medical institutions, affiliated or seeking affiliation;‖ (emphasis supplied) The territorial jurisdiction of UHS has been given in Section 5 of the Ordinance 2002 which is as under: ―5. Jurisdiction of the University.– The University shall exercise the powers conferred on it by or under this Ordinance within the territorial limits in the Province of the Punjab and in respect of the constituent colleges: Provided that Government may, in consultation with the University, by general or special order, modify the extent and scope of the aforesaid powers of the University with regard to such territorial limits or institutions. The University may admit to its privileges under prescribed conditions, a medical institution falling within territorial limits of another University, whether inside or outside Pakistan; provided that the consent of such other University and the sanction of Government is first obtained.‖ (emphasis supplied) Under Section 27 (ii) of the Ordinance 2002, the Syndicate of the UHS has been authorized to affiliate or disaffiliate medical institutions.

Jurisdiction of the University.– The University shall exercise the powers conferred on it by or under this Ordinance within the territorial limits in the Province of the Punjab and in respect of the constituent colleges: Provided that Government may, in consultation with the University, by general or special order, modify the extent and scope of the aforesaid powers of the University with regard to such territorial limits or institutions. The University may admit to its privileges under prescribed conditions, a medical institution falling within territorial limits of another University, whether inside or outside Pakistan; provided that the consent of such other University and the sanction of Government is first obtained.‖ (emphasis supplied) Under Section 27 (ii) of the Ordinance 2002, the Syndicate of the UHS has been authorized to affiliate or disaffiliate medical institutions. The said provision is reproduced as under: ―27. Powers and duties of the Syndicate.–.... Without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing powers and subject to the provisions of this Ordinance and the Statutes, the Syndicate shall have the powers to– (i)..... (ii) affiliate or disaffiliate medical institutions;‖ (emphasis supplied) Writ Petition No.29214/2011 7 The formation of Academic Council of UHS, its powers and duties have been given in Section 30 and Section 31 of the Ordinance 2002, which are reproduced as under: ―30. Academic Council.– The Academic Council shall consist of– (i) the Vice Chancellor, who shall be its Chairman; (ii) the Deans of the University; (iii) the Directors of Institutes; (iv) the Principals of affiliated and constituent colleges; (v) the Chairpersons of the teaching departments; (vi) the Professors including Professors Emeritus; (vii) five persons to be nominated by the Chancellor; (viii) the Registrar; (ix) the Controller of Examinations; and (x) the University Librarian. The members of the Academic Council, other than ex- officio members, shall hold office for three years, and if the office of any such member becomes vacant before the expiry of such term, such vacancy shall be filled in the prescribed manner. The quorum for a meeting of the Academic Council shall be one third of the total number of members, a fraction being counted as one. 31. Powers and duties of the Academic Council.– The Academic Council shall be the academic body of the University and shall, subject to the provisions of this Ordinance, the Statutes and the Regulations, have the powers to lay down proper standards of instruction, research, publication, examination, and to regulate and promote the academic life of the University, medical institutions, and affiliated and constituent colleges.

The quorum for a meeting of the Academic Council shall be one third of the total number of members, a fraction being counted as one. 31. Powers and duties of the Academic Council.– The Academic Council shall be the academic body of the University and shall, subject to the provisions of this Ordinance, the Statutes and the Regulations, have the powers to lay down proper standards of instruction, research, publication, examination, and to regulate and promote the academic life of the University, medical institutions, and affiliated and constituent colleges. Without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing powers and subject to the provisions of this Ordinance, the Statutes and the Regulations, the Academic Council shall have the powers to– (i) advise the Syndicate on academic matters; (ii) regulate the conduct of teaching, research, publications and examinations; (iii) regulate the admission of students to the University and conduct examinations of affiliated colleges and medical institutions; (iv) regulate the award of scholarships, medals and prizes; (v) regulate the conduct and discipline of students of the University, medical institutions and affiliated colleges; (vi) propose to the Syndicate schemes for the constitution and organization of faculties, institutes and other academic bodies; (vii) make Regulations on the recommendations of the Boards of Faculties and the Boards of Studies for approval of the Board; Writ Petition No.29214/2011 8 (viii) propose Regulations for each academic year, on the recommendations of the Boards of Studies of the University, prescribing the courses of studies, the syllabi and the outlines of tests for all examinations; provided that, if the recommendations of a Board of Faculties or the Board of Studies are not received by the specified date, the Academic Council may, subject to the approval of the Syndicate permit such Regulations to continue for the next following year; (ix) recognize the examinations of other Universities or examining bodies as equivalent to the corresponding examinations of the University; (x) appoint members to the various Authorities in accordance with the provisions of this Ordinance; and (xi) perform such other functions as may be prescribed by the Statutes.‖ (emphasis supplied) Under Section 34 (iv) of the Ordinance 2002, the Syndicate of the UHS has been authorized to make Statutes to prescribe and regulate the matters regarding affiliation and disaffiliation of medical institutions.

Without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing powers and subject to the provisions of this Ordinance, the Statutes and the Regulations, the Academic Council shall have the powers to– (i) advise the Syndicate on academic matters; (ii) regulate the conduct of teaching, research, publications and examinations; (iii) regulate the admission of students to the University and conduct examinations of affiliated colleges and medical institutions; (iv) regulate the award of scholarships, medals and prizes; (v) regulate the conduct and discipline of students of the University, medical institutions and affiliated colleges; (vi) propose to the Syndicate schemes for the constitution and organization of faculties, institutes and other academic bodies; (vii) make Regulations on the recommendations of the Boards of Faculties and the Boards of Studies for approval of the Board; Writ Petition No.29214/2011 8 (viii) propose Regulations for each academic year, on the recommendations of the Boards of Studies of the University, prescribing the courses of studies, the syllabi and the outlines of tests for all examinations; provided that, if the recommendations of a Board of Faculties or the Board of Studies are not received by the specified date, the Academic Council may, subject to the approval of the Syndicate permit such Regulations to continue for the next following year; (ix) recognize the examinations of other Universities or examining bodies as equivalent to the corresponding examinations of the University; (x) appoint members to the various Authorities in accordance with the provisions of this Ordinance; and (xi) perform such other functions as may be prescribed by the Statutes.‖ (emphasis supplied) Under Section 34 (iv) of the Ordinance 2002, the Syndicate of the UHS has been authorized to make Statutes to prescribe and regulate the matters regarding affiliation and disaffiliation of medical institutions. For reference, aforesaid provision is reproduced as under: ―34. Statutes.– Subject to the provisions of this Ordinance, Statutes may be made to prescribe and regulate all or any of the following matters:–.... (iv) the affiliation and disaffiliation of medical institutions and related matters‖ Pursuant to Section 34(iv) of the Ordinance 2002 Statutes for Affiliation of Medical Institutions of 2011 was promulgated (which was repealed upon promulgation of Statutes for Affiliation of Medical Institutions 2022) wherein a procedure is provided for application to be filed by all medical institutions seeking affiliation with UHS. Section 37 of the Ordinance ibid requires mandatory affiliation of all the medical colleges/ institutions, whether in public or private sector, located within the geographical boundaries of the province of the Punjab with the UHS. For reference, aforesaid provision is reproduced as under: ―37. Affiliation of medical institutions.– All medical institutions, whether in public or private sector, located within the geographical boundaries of the province of the Punjab, whether affiliated with any other University, Examination Writ Petition No.29214/2011 9 Board or a Medical Faculty, notwithstanding anything contained in any other law for the time being in force, shall affiliate with the University within such period and on such terms and conditions, as may be prescribed.

For reference, aforesaid provision is reproduced as under: ―37. Affiliation of medical institutions.– All medical institutions, whether in public or private sector, located within the geographical boundaries of the province of the Punjab, whether affiliated with any other University, Examination Writ Petition No.29214/2011 9 Board or a Medical Faculty, notwithstanding anything contained in any other law for the time being in force, shall affiliate with the University within such period and on such terms and conditions, as may be prescribed. A medical institution seeking affiliation to the University shall make an application to the University and shall satisfy it that– (i) the medical institution is under the management of Government or a regularly constituted governing body; (ii) the financial resources of the medical institution are sufficient to ensure its continued maintenance and efficient working; (iii) the strength and qualifications and the terms and conditions of service of the teaching and other staff of the medical institution are satisfactory for the purpose of teaching of prescribed courses of studies and training in the institution; (iv) the medical institution has framed proper rules governing the conduct and discipline of its employees; (v) the building of the medical institution is suitable and commodious for its requirements; (vi) the medical institution has, or can make a provision in the prescribed manner for the residence of students, not residing with their parents or guardians, and their supervision, and physical and general welfare; (vii) the medical institution has a provision for a library and adequate library service; (viii) the medical institution is properly equipped with laboratories, museums and other places of practical work required for courses of studies taught in the medical institution; (ix) the medical institution has, or can make, provision for the residence of its Vice Chancellor and Deputy Dean, and other members of the teaching staff in the medical institution building or near such building or the residence of students; and (x) the affiliation of the medical institution shall not be injurious to the education or discipline of the students of other institutions in the neighborhood.

Affiliation of medical institutions.– All medical institutions, whether in public or private sector, located within the geographical boundaries of the province of the Punjab, whether affiliated with any other University, Examination Writ Petition No.29214/2011 9 Board or a Medical Faculty, notwithstanding anything contained in any other law for the time being in force, shall affiliate with the University within such period and on such terms and conditions, as may be prescribed. A medical institution seeking affiliation to the University shall make an application to the University and shall satisfy it that– (i) the medical institution is under the management of Government or a regularly constituted governing body; (ii) the financial resources of the medical institution are sufficient to ensure its continued maintenance and efficient working; (iii) the strength and qualifications and the terms and conditions of service of the teaching and other staff of the medical institution are satisfactory for the purpose of teaching of prescribed courses of studies and training in the institution; (iv) the medical institution has framed proper rules governing the conduct and discipline of its employees; (v) the building of the medical institution is suitable and commodious for its requirements; (vi) the medical institution has, or can make a provision in the prescribed manner for the residence of students, not residing with their parents or guardians, and their supervision, and physical and general welfare; (vii) the medical institution has a provision for a library and adequate library service; (viii) the medical institution is properly equipped with laboratories, museums and other places of practical work required for courses of studies taught in the medical institution; (ix) the medical institution has, or can make, provision for the residence of its Vice Chancellor and Deputy Dean, and other members of the teaching staff in the medical institution building or near such building or the residence of students; and (x) the affiliation of the medical institution shall not be injurious to the education or discipline of the students of other institutions in the neighborhood. The application shall further contain an undertaking by the medical institution that after the institution is affiliated, any change in the management or the teaching staff shall forthwith be reported to the University and that the teaching staff shall possess such qualifications as are, or may be, prescribed. The Syndicate shall, after considering the recommendations of the Affiliation Committee, dispose of the application for affiliation in accordance with such procedure, as may be prescribed, and may grant or refuse affiliation: Provided that the affiliation shall not be refused, unless the medical institution has been given an opportunity of making a representation against the proposed decision.‖ (emphasis supplied) 9. Under Section 37 of the Ordinance 2002, it is mandatory that all medical colleges or institutions, whether in public or Writ Petition No.29214/2011 10 private sector, located within the territorial boundaries of the Province of Punjab, whether affiliated with any other University, Examination Board or a Medical Faculty, notwithstanding anything contained in any other law for the time being in force, shall affiliate with UHS. It is not out of place to mention here that the question of mandatory affiliation of medical colleges with UHS was earlier arisen in the year 2003, when concurrent list was still in vogue, a few of the medical colleges/ universities/students challenged the vires of Ordinance 2002 and a learned Full Bench of this Court in its reported judgment titled as Ahmad Abdullah and 62 others Vs. Government of the Punjab and 3 others (PLD 2003 Lahore 752), has declared provision of Section 37 of the Ordinance 2002 as mandatory and thus all the public or private medical colleges or institutions operating within the territorial boundaries of the Province of Punjab shall have to get affiliation with UHS who on receipt of an application of the institution for affiliation, shall pass an order as per law.

It is not out of place to mention here that the question of mandatory affiliation of medical colleges with UHS was earlier arisen in the year 2003, when concurrent list was still in vogue, a few of the medical colleges/ universities/students challenged the vires of Ordinance 2002 and a learned Full Bench of this Court in its reported judgment titled as Ahmad Abdullah and 62 others Vs. Government of the Punjab and 3 others (PLD 2003 Lahore 752), has declared provision of Section 37 of the Ordinance 2002 as mandatory and thus all the public or private medical colleges or institutions operating within the territorial boundaries of the Province of Punjab shall have to get affiliation with UHS who on receipt of an application of the institution for affiliation, shall pass an order as per law. In the judgment (supra) it is held as under: ―16. A comparative study of the two Statutes would show that while the Federal Law (Medical Council Ordinance, 1962) contemplates that creation of a Central Regulatory Authority for whole Pakistan (Medical Council) to establish a uniform minimum standard of basic and higher qualifications in medicine and dentistry‖, The Provincial Law (University of Health Sciences, Ordinance, 2002) seeks to establish a degree awarding University, with power to undertake, research, teaching, prescribe courses of studies to be taught in affiliated and constituent colleges.‖...... 21. A close look at subsection of section 37 would indicate that it makes mandatory for all medical institutions except those which have been established under some Federal Statue and are under the Administrative control of the Federal Government within the geographical boundaries of the Province of Punjab to affiliate with the newly establish University of Health Sciences and ―within such period‖ and on ―such terms and conditions‖ as may be prescribed. The institutions run by the Federal Government can, of course, voluntarily affiliate. The time frame was considered essential by the law maker as an abrupt switch-over from one University to the other could be problematic either for the students or the institutions concerned. Subsection of this section is relatable to those institutions which already stand affiliated with a Writ Petition No.29214/2011 11 University and qua whom terms and conditions and terms of affiliation are to be prescribed, whereas subsection stipulates the basic requirements of eligibility of affiliation for an institution/applicant, having no prior affiliation.

The time frame was considered essential by the law maker as an abrupt switch-over from one University to the other could be problematic either for the students or the institutions concerned. Subsection of this section is relatable to those institutions which already stand affiliated with a Writ Petition No.29214/2011 11 University and qua whom terms and conditions and terms of affiliation are to be prescribed, whereas subsection stipulates the basic requirements of eligibility of affiliation for an institution/applicant, having no prior affiliation. Subsection of the same section vests the power to allow or refuse affiliation with the Syndicate and after considering the recommendations of the Affiliation Committee. In the case of the former category of intuitions, it is mandatory both for institutions and University of Health Sciences to affiliate whereas for the latter it is discretionary with the UHS to grant or refuse affiliation.‖ (emphasis supplied) The final declaration is prescribed in paragraph No.44 of the judgment (supra), which is as under:- ―44.......... (iii) That the letter dated 2-12-2002 issued by the Vice-Chancellor of University of Health Sciences, insofar as it declares the medical colleges to be deemed to have been affiliated, is without any lawful authority. The letter shall be considered merely as an intimation to the medical institutions concerned about the change in law qua affiliation and an invitation to comply with the mandate of law. The intimation sent by the Principals of the medical colleges/ institutions would be treated as applications for affiliation and the latter shall pass appropriate orders for affiliation if it has not already been passed, without further loss of time in terms of section 37 of the University of Health Sciences Ordinance, 2002. (emphasis supplied) The judgment (supra) was upheld by the Hon'ble Supreme Court of Pakistan which pronouncement has been reported as Shafique Ahmed and others Vs. Government of Punjab and others (PLD 2004 SC 168). As such, the matter of mandatory affiliation of the medical College/Institution with UHS as well as the jurisdiction through enactments as well as by judicial dicta has finally been settled.

In this respect Justice G. P. Singh has aptly explained:- “A clause beginning with „notwithstanding anything contained in this Act or in some particular provision in the Act or in some particular Act or in any law for the time being in force‟, is sometimes appended to a section in the beginning, with a view to give the enacting part of the section in case of conflict an overriding effect over the provision or Act mentioned in the non obstante clause. It is equivalent to saying that in spite of the provision or Act mentioned in the non obstante clause, the enactment following it will have its full operation or that the provisions embraced in the non obstante clause will not be an impediment for the operation of the enactment.” In the judgment reported as Packages Limited through its General Manager and others v.

It is equivalent to saying that in spite of the provision or Act mentioned in the non obstante clause, the enactment following it will have its full operation or that the provisions embraced in the non obstante clause will not be an impediment for the operation of the enactment.” In the judgment reported as Packages Limited through its General Manager and others v. Muhammad Maqbool and others (PLD 1991 SC 258) this Court observed:- “In our opinion a „non obstante‟ clause operates as an ouster of the earlier provisions only where there is a conflict and inconsistency between the earlier provisions and those contained in the later provision and, therefore, must be read in the context in which it is operating. Accordingly, a non obstante clause will operate as ouster only if an inconsistency between the two is found to exist.” In the judgment reported as Muhammad Mohsin Ghuman and others v. Government of Punjab through Home Secretary, Lahore and others (2013 SCMR 85), this Court cited with approval a passage from Interpretation of Statutes by N. S. Bhindra which reads as under:- “It has to be read in the context of what the legislature conveys in the enacting part of the provision. It should first be ascertained what the enacting part of the section provides on a fair construction of words used according to their natural and ordinary meaning and the non obstante clause is to be understood as operating to set aside as no longer valid anything contained in relevant existing law which is inconsistent with the new enactment. The enacting part of a statute must, where it is clear, be taken to control the non obstante clause where both cannot be read harmoniously, for even apart from such clause a later law abrogates earlier laws clearly inconsistent with it. The meaning of the enacting part which is so ascertained is then to be taken as overriding anything inconsistent to that meaning in the provisions mentioned in the non obstante clause. A non obstante clause is usually used in a provision to indicate that that provision should prevail despite anything to the contrary in the provision mentioned in such non obstante clause. In case there is any inconsistency between the non obstante clause and another provision one of the objects of such a clause is to indicate that it is the non obstante clause which would prevail over the other clauses. It does not, however, necessarily mean that there must be repugnancy between the two provisions in all such cases. The principle underlying non obstante clause may be invoked only in the case of „irreconcilable conflict‟.” From the above it is clear that the non obstante clause of section 4 of the Ordinance, 2001 has been used by the legislature to give the provisions of the said Ordinance an overriding effect over any other law for the time being in force which may be contrary thereto. The use of the word ̳notwithstanding‘ in section 4 ibid indicates the legislative intent to avoid the operation of conflicting provisions, by providing that in the event of such conflict, the provisions of the Ordinance, 2001 would take precedence over any such inconsistent law.‖ 11. Moreover, after promulgation of the above said 18th amendment, the subject of education comes within the exclusive legislative domain of the provinces, thus, any Federal Department/Authority lacks jurisdiction to intrude into the domain of the provincial authorities or impede or frustrate the objective and spirit of the law. Therefore, the government functionaries [as in this case the Ministry of Inter Provincial Coordination (Health Wing), Government of Pakistan] are debarred from performing any act in deviation to the mandatory statutory provision of Section 37 of Ordinance 2002, allowing a medical College to escape from the mandatory affiliation, whereas the above process has already been endorsed by the Hon'ble Supreme Court of Pakistan in Shafique Ahmad Case (supra). I may point out that under Section 37 of UHS Act, Azra Naheed Medical College, Lahore, can only be affiliated with University of Health Sciences, Lahore since it is located within the geographical boundaries of the Province of Punjab. The concerned Section 37 of the University of Health Sciences, Lahore Act is reproduced below for your kind perusal, ―All medical institutions, whether in public or private sector, located within the geographical boundaries of the province of the Punjab, whether affiliated with any other University, Examination Board or a Medical Faculty, notwithstanding anything contained in any other law for the time being in force, shall affiliate with the University within such period and on such terms and conditions, as may be prescribed.” Honorable Lahore High Court, Lahore reinforced the UHS (Amended) Act 2009 by stating that, “That subject to Article 143 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, all the medical institutions Writ Petition No.29214/2011 27 under the administrative control of the Provincial Government and the medical institutions in the private sector in the territorial limits of Punjab are under a mandate of the new law to affiliate with the University of Health Sciences within such time and no such terms and conditions which may be prescribed in term of section 5 of the Ordinance” (Writ Petition # 7281/2003, Ahmad Abdullah vs Government of Punjab) It is pertinent to mention here that University issued NOC to Azra Naheed Medical College, Lahore on its own request and accordingly the college is obligated under the above mentioned provisions of law to submit the affiliation request for visit of Affiliation Committee of UHS.

Reliance is placed on the cases titled as A.R. Khan Vs. P.N. Boga through LR (PLD 1987 SC 107) and Overseas Pakistanis Foundation & Others Vs. Sqn. Ldr. (Retd.) Syed Mukhtar Ali Shah & Another (2007 SCMR 569). Lastly, it is significant to mention here that the Superior College is a creation of Provincial Legislation and it has to abide by all the provincial laws, rules, regulations and is debarred from bypassing the mandatory provisions of law. Further any federal authority is also patently debarred to pass any prejudicial order to the spirit of Ordinance 2002 and if any order is so passed that would be in-effective, inoperative and without jurisdiction qua the provincial enactment. Writ Petition No.29214/2011 32 15. In view of the foregoing discussion, there is no cavil and cudgel in issuance of a declaration that every medical College / institution which is rendering medical education/training to the MBBS students within the boarder / precincts of Province of Punjab has to necessarily get itself affiliated with the UHS before initiating teaching/training process under the spirit of the 18th constitutional amendment and law laid down by the Hon‘ble Supreme Court of Pakistan as well as mandatory statutory requirement prescribed in Section 37 of the Ordinance 2002. 16. Now adverting to the issue agitated in W.P.No.19848/2011 wherein the Superior College has challenged the notice issued by the UHS for mandatory affiliation as well as publication of notice in media for the knowledge of the general public regarding unwarranted and unauthorized imparting of medical education. As discussed above, admittedly the college was not affiliated with UHS, who being a legal guardian of the students to protect them from loss of precious time, educational career and loss of money through misrepresentation made by the college, was / is well within jurisdiction to preemptively take stringent precautionary positive measures to circulate widespread awareness in the public regarding foul, arbitrary, farcical and misrepresentation of fake educational activities of the educationist institution or the administration of a concerned College to safeguard the public and students.

Now adverting to the issue agitated in W.P.No.19848/2011 wherein the Superior College has challenged the notice issued by the UHS for mandatory affiliation as well as publication of notice in media for the knowledge of the general public regarding unwarranted and unauthorized imparting of medical education. As discussed above, admittedly the college was not affiliated with UHS, who being a legal guardian of the students to protect them from loss of precious time, educational career and loss of money through misrepresentation made by the college, was / is well within jurisdiction to preemptively take stringent precautionary positive measures to circulate widespread awareness in the public regarding foul, arbitrary, farcical and misrepresentation of fake educational activities of the educationist institution or the administration of a concerned College to safeguard the public and students. Besides above, as the college has come to this Court with unclean hands, thus it is disentitled for any discretionary relief, as such, it is observed that UHS was well within jurisdiction to publish notice against contumacious institution who denies affiliation with the UHS. Moreover, only vires of notice has been challenged in the writ petition by the Superior College whereas ordinarily writ petition is not maintainable against the notice. Therefore, on both counts writ petition of college deserves dismissal. Writ Petition No.29214/2011 33 17. In view of the foregoing, the notification dated 13.08.2011 impugned in this writ petition issued by respondent No.1 is found to be in violation of the spirit of 18th constitutional amendment, law laid down by the Hon‘ble Supreme Court of Pakistan as well as mandatory statutory requirement prescribed in Section 37 of the Ordinance 2002. Resultantly, this Writ Petition [No.29214/2011] is allowed and the impugned notification dated 13.08.2011 issued by the Ministry of Inter Provincial Coordination (Health Wing), Government of Pakistan is hereby set aside whereas the connected Writ Petition [No.19848/2011] is hereby dismissed.
